title: राहु-केतु स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राहु-केतु स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ राहु स्तोत्रम् ॥

अर्धकायं महावीर्यं चन्द्रादित्यविमर्दनम्।
सिंहिकागर्भसम्भूतं तं राहुं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ १॥

रहुर्दानवमन्त्री च सिंहिकाचित्तनन्दनः।
अर्धकायः सदा क्रोधी चन्द्रादित्यविमर्दनः॥ २॥

रौद्रो रुद्रप्रियो दैत्यः स्वर्भानुर्भानुभीतिदः।
ग्रहराजः सुधापायी राकातिथ्यभिलाषुकः॥ ३॥

कालदृष्टिः कालरूपः श्रीकण्ठहृदयाश्रयः।
विधुन्तुदो धूम्रवर्णः शतमन्यु वरप्रदः॥ ४॥

कृत्तिकानक्षत्रजातो मन्दसौख्यप्रदो महान्।
चन्दनालेपभूषाढ्यो नीलाम्बरविभूषणः॥ ५॥

द्वादशनामानि राहोर्यः पठेच्छ्रद्धयान्वितः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वरोगविवर्जितः॥ ६॥

॥ केतु स्तोत्रम् ॥

पलाशपुष्पसंकाशं तारकाग्रहमस्तकम्।
रौद्रं रौद्रात्मकं घोरं तं केतुं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

केतुर्ग्रहपतिः क्रूरः शिखी धूम्रवपुः प्रभुः।
चित्रवर्णो ग्रहश्रेष्ठः पुच्छलो मोक्षदो महान्॥ ८॥

धूम्राकारो धूम्रवर्णो ज्वालाजिह्वो भयंकरः।
लाङ्गूलो ग्रहकूटस्थो विशालाक्षो भयप्रदः॥ ९॥

चित्रगुप्तजनप्रियः प्रेतराजसमो बली।
एकादशनामानि केतोर्यः पठते सदा।
सर्वग्रहकृता पीडा नश्यते नात्र संशयः॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री राहु-केतु स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Rahu-Ketu Stotram ||

|| Rahu Stotram ||

Ardha Kayam Mahaviryam Chandraditya Vimardanam |
Simhika Garbha Sambhutam Tam Rahum Pranamamy Aham || 1 ||

Rahur Danava Mantri Cha Simhika Chitta Nandanah |
Ardha Kayah Sada Krodhi Chandraditya Vimardanah || 2 ||

Raudro Rudra Priyo Daityah Svarbhanur Bhanu Bhitidah |
Graha Rajah Sudhapaayi Raka Tithyabhilashukah || 3 ||

Kala Drishtih Kala Rupah Shrikantha Hridayashrayah |
Vidhuntudo Dhumravarnah Shatamanyu Varapradah || 4 ||

Krittika Nakshatra Jato Manda Saukhya Prado Mahan |
Chandanalep Bhushadhyo Nilambara Vibhushanah || 5 ||

Dvadasha Namani Rahor Yah Pathech Chraddhaya Anvitah |
Sarva Papa Vinirmuktah Sarva Roga Vivarjitah || 6 ||

|| Ketu Stotram ||

Palasha Pushpa Sankasham Taraka Graha Mastakam |
Raudram Raudratmakam Ghoram Tam Ketum Pranamamy Aham || 7 ||

Ketur Grahapatih Krurah Shikhi Dhumra Vapuh Prabhuh |
Chitra Varno Graha Shreshthah Puchchhalo Mokshado Mahan || 8 ||

Dhumrakaro Dhumravarnah Jvala Jihvo Bhayamkarah |
Langulo Graha Kutastho Vishalaksho Bhayapradah || 9 ||

Chitragupta Jana Priyah Preta Raja Samo Bali |
Ekadasha Namani Ketor Yah Pathate Sada |
Sarva Graha Krita Pida Nashyate Natra Samshayah || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Rahu-Ketu Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
राहु स्तोत्र: अर्धकाय (आधे शरीर वाले), महापराक्रमी, चन्द्र-सूर्य को ग्रसने वाले, सिंहिका के गर्भ से उत्पन्न राहु को प्रणाम।

राहु दानवों के मन्त्री, सिंहिका के हृदय को आनन्दित करने वाले, सदा क्रोधी, रौद्र, रुद्रप्रिय, स्वर्भानु, ग्रहराज, सुधापायी (अमृत पीने वाले), कालदृष्टि, कालरूप, शिव के हृदय में निवास करने वाले, धूम्रवर्ण हैं।

जो श्रद्धापूर्वक राहु के बारह नामों का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वपापमुक्त और सर्वरोगरहित हो जाता है।

केतु स्तोत्र: पलाश पुष्प के समान, तारकाग्रह के शिखर पर स्थित, रौद्र, घोर केतु को प्रणाम। केतु ग्रहपति, क्रूर, शिखी (ध्वजधारी), धूम्रवपु, चित्रवर्ण, पुच्छधारी, मोक्षदाता हैं।

जो सदा केतु के ग्यारह नामों का पाठ करता है, उसकी सर्व ग्रहजनित पीड़ा नष्ट होती है।
benefits: Combined Rahu and Ketu stotra for complete shadow planet remedies
12 names of Rahu destroy all sins and diseases
11 names of Ketu remove all planetary afflictions
Essential during Rahu-Ketu Dasha and transits
Traditionally invoked for protection from sudden calamities, accidents and hidden enemies
description: The Rahu Ketu Stotram, known in Sanskrit as राहु-केतु स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of the Nine Planets (Navagraha). Attributed to Skanda Purana (Graha Khanda), this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to combined Rahu and Ketu stotra for complete shadow planet remedies and 11 names of Ketu remove all planetary afflictions. It is also said to protect from sudden calamities, accidents and hidden enemies. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Grahan Puja to invoke divine grace. With 10 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Rahu Ketu Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Rahu Ketu Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Combined stotra with 12 Rahu names and 11 Ketu names for planetary remedy.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Rahu Ketu Stotram and why are the two planets prayed together?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rahu Ketu Stotram is a combined Sanskrit hymn that joins the praise verses of Rahu and Ketu, the two chhaya-grahas (shadow planets) of the Vedic navagraha system. They are always invoked together because puranic cosmology describes them as the severed head and trunk of the same asura Svarbhanu, who drank the amrita during the samudra-manthana and was beheaded by Vishnu's sudarshana-chakra. Since the two halves remain alive as immortal nodal points, jyotisha treats them as a single axis and prescribes their combined recitation for any Rahu-Ketu axial affliction, Kaal Sarp Dosh, or eclipse-related grahan dosha. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang Navagraha; Vishnu Purana 1.9.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Kaal Sarp Dosh and how does this stotra address it?
seoFaqs.answer: Kaal Sarp Dosh is the astrological condition in which all seven traditional planets (Surya through Shani) are confined between the Rahu-Ketu axis in a horoscope, leaving no planet outside the nodal corridor. Traditional jyotisha prescribes the combined Rahu Ketu Stotram, recited 108 times along with the Kaal Sarp Dosh nivaran mantra, as one of the principal household remedies. Pilgrimage to Trimbakeshwar (Nashik) for the Kaal Sarp Dosh puja, to Kalahasti (Andhra Pradesh) for the Rahu-Ketu sarpa-dosha homam, and to Naganatha temples are recommended in conjunction. The stotra is the daily recitation and the temple puja the periodic intensive remedy. (Sources: Drikpanchang Kaal Sarp Dosh; Kalahasti temple guides; AstroSage.)

seoFaqs.question: How are Rahu and Ketu described in the iconography of this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotra describes Rahu as ardhakaya (only the upper half of a body), of dark blue or smoky complexion, born from the womb of Simhika, four-armed, holding a sword and shield, seated on a vulture or a chariot drawn by eight black horses, wearing blue garments and ornaments of gomeda (hessonite). Ketu is described as the headless lower half, of grey or smoky complexion, holding a mace and showing varada-mudra, seated on a vulture or serpent, ornamented with cat's-eye (vaidurya). Together they form a single Svarbhanu-axis whose iconography emphasises the shadowy, indirect, karmic nature of these grahas. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Rahu Ketu Stotram; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Rahu Ketu Stotram be chanted for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday is the day prescribed for Rahu and Tuesday for Ketu, but the combined Rahu Ketu Stotram is most powerfully recited during Rahu Kalam and Yamagandam on these days, on the eighteen-month Rahu-Ketu peyarchi transit days, on lunar and solar eclipses, and on Naga Panchami and Naga Chaturthi. Devotees facing Rahu mahadasha, Ketu mahadasha, the Rahu-Ketu axis transiting their natal Moon, or those born under Ardra, Swati, Shatabhisha (Rahu) or Ashwini, Magha, Mula (Ketu) nakshatras are advised to recite it eleven, twenty-one or one hundred and eight times. (Sources: Drikpanchang transit calendar; AstroSage Rahu remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the phalashruti of Rahu Ketu Stotram promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti declares that the combined hymn destroys Rahu-dosha, Ketu-dosha and Kaal Sarp Dosh, removes obstacles in marriage, progeny, career and travel that arise from nodal afflictions, and protects against accidents, poisoning, snake-bite, sudden reversals, debts and litigation. It is also said to cure ailments associated with the chhaya-grahas — chronic skin diseases, mysterious fevers, allergies, anxiety disorders, addictions, and conditions doctors cannot diagnose. Spiritually, it is said to liberate the chanter from past-life karmic patterns indicated by the Rahu-Ketu axis. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi phalashruti; AstroSage Rahu Ketu remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: What gemstones, mantras and offerings accompany Rahu Ketu Stotram recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Jyotisha pairs the stotra with the beej mantras Om Bhram Bhrim Bhraum Sah Rahave Namah and Om Sram Srim Sraum Sah Ketave Namah, japa with a hessonite or cat's-eye mala or with rudraksha. Offerings include black sesame, urad dal, mustard oil, blue or grey cloth, iron or lead items for Rahu, and multi-coloured cloth, coconut, bananas and sandalwood for Ketu. Wearing a hessonite (gomeda) for Rahu and a cat's-eye (vaidurya) for Ketu set in silver after prana-pratishtha is the prescribed gemstone remedy. Donations on Saturdays for Rahu and on Tuesdays for Ketu are recommended along with feeding crows and dogs. (Sources: Drikpanchang Rahu Saturday remedies; AstroSage.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Rahu Ketu Stotram different from separate Rahu Stotra and Ketu Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rahu Stotra and Ketu Stotra are independent hymns each consisting of eight to nine verses preserved in the Skanda Purana navagraha cycle, recited individually for single-planet afflictions. The Rahu Ketu Stotram combines selected verses from both, joined into a single recitation specifically for axial afflictions where both nodes are involved — Kaal Sarp Dosh, eclipse dosha, or Rahu-Ketu mahadasha-antardasha overlaps. For comprehensive nodal pacification, the combined version is chanted; for targeted single-node remediation, the individual stotras are used. Many devotees recite the combined version daily and the individual stotras on the respective Rahu and Ketu days. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Navagraha texts; Drikpanchang Rahu Ketu remedies.)
